login_credentials.note: Requires ADMIN role user to access /ai/admin/* routes

prerequisite_seed_data: 3 AiModel records (claude-opus-4-6, claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-haiku-4-5) seeded from E5
Multiple AiSkill records grouped by moduleKey seeded from E5/E5b
1 example automation 'Daily AR Aging Summary' seeded from E5c-1
6 system AiPromptVariable records seeded from E5c-1

journeys.name: AI Configuration Dashboard
journeys.description: Verify the AI Configuration Dashboard loads with summary cards, token usage chart, and quick-nav links (E5c-3 AC-1, E5c-5 AC-10, E5c-6 AC-6)

steps.visual_check: Dashboard page with 'AI Configuration' heading, 4 summary cards visible (Active Models, Active Agents, Active Skills, Automations), purple-themed Concept D styling with #f4f2ff background

steps.expected_result: Card shows model count in mono font with monthly cost subtitle
Card shows total skill count with 'across N modules' subtitle
Card shows active count (green badge) and paused count (amber badge)
Line chart visible with 'Token Usage (Last 30 Days)' heading, x-axis dates, y-axis tokens
Quick-nav cards for Model Registry, Prompt Templates, and Automations are visible with clickable links
Automation Health section visible below quick-nav with: automations by status chart, failed runs count, upcoming scheduled runs list, token spend trend chart
steps.visual_check: Automation Health section showing donut chart for automation statuses, failed runs badge, upcoming runs list, and 7-day token spend area chart. All cards have 12px radius and purple-tinted shadows

journeys.name: Model Registry CRUD Lifecycle
journeys.description: Create, view, edit, and delete an AI model through the Model Registry UI (E5c-3 AC-2, AC-3)

steps.visual_check: T1 Entity List with 'Model Registry' heading, breadcrumbs showing 'AI Administration > Model Registry', table columns: name (mono), provider, model ID (mono), max tokens (mono), cost/M input (mono), cost/M output (mono), routing tags (purple badges), active status, default flag. At least 3 seeded models visible

steps.expected_result: Seeded model 'claude-opus-4-6' is visible in the list
Form page loads with 'New Model' heading, Primary and Advanced tabs visible, Save and Cancel buttons in action bar
steps.visual_check: Model form with fields: Name, Display Name, Provider dropdown, Model ID, Max Input Tokens, Max Output Tokens, Cost fields. Save button is disabled (form not dirty). Concept D styling applied

steps.expected_result: Form fields populated, Save button becomes enabled
Advanced tab content shows: Routing Tags, Capabilities JSON editor, Fallback Model dropdown, Config JSON editor
Tags appear as purple pill badges, fallback model selected

steps.visual_check: Success toast 'Model created' visible. Page navigates to the model detail/edit page showing the saved data. Breadcrumbs show 'AI Administration > Model Registry > Test GPT-4o'

steps.expected_result: Field updated, Save button becomes enabled
Model updated successfully, success toast visible
Model list shows the new model with updated display name
New model visible in the list with mono font name
Confirmation dialog appears asking to confirm deletion
Model deleted, toast shows success, model removed from list
steps.visual_check: Model list no longer contains 'test-gpt-4o'. Success toast visible

journeys.name: Model Business Rules Enforcement
journeys.description: Test model business rules: default model toggle, deactivation guard, circular fallback rejection (E5c-3 AC-3)

steps.expected_result: Model list loads with seeded models

steps.target: Row for the model marked as Default
steps.expected_result: Model edit form opens for the default model
422 error: 'Cannot deactivate the default model. Assign a new default first.'
steps.visual_check: Error toast or inline error message visible explaining that the default model cannot be deactivated

steps.expected_result: Attempting to create circular fallback chain: opus -> fallback-test-a -> opus

steps.visual_check: Error toast or inline error about circular fallback detected

journeys.name: Prompt Template CRUD with Versioning
journeys.description: Create a prompt, edit it (triggering version creation), view version history, and restore an old version (E5c-3 AC-4, AC-5, AC-6, AC-7)

steps.visual_check: T1 Entity List with 'Prompt Templates' heading, columns: name (mono), category (coloured badge), active version (mono 'vN'), variable count, active status, last updated. At least 6 seeded prompts visible with category badges (purple, blue, amber, green, etc.)

steps.expected_result: Editor loads with metadata section, System Prompt editor, User Template editor, Parameters editor. No version sidebar (create mode)
steps.visual_check: Prompt editor page with 'New Prompt Template' heading, fields for Name, Description, Category dropdown, Active toggle. Two code editor areas: 'System Prompt' and 'User Template' with mono font. Concept D styling

input_data.Name: test-e2e-prompt
input_data.Description: E2E test prompt for automated testing
input_data.System Prompt: You are an AI assistant helping with {{company.name}} data analysis. Today is {{today}}.

steps.visual_check: Success toast visible. Page shows the saved prompt. Version sidebar now visible showing 'v1' as the only version with changeReason 'Initial version'. Active version badge shows 'v1'

input_data.System Prompt: You are an AI assistant helping with {{company.name}} data analysis. Today is {{today}}. You specialise in financial insights.

steps.visual_check: Dialog/modal with title 'Save Prompt Changes', textarea for change reason, Cancel and Save buttons

input_data.Change Reason: Added financial specialisation to system prompt

steps.target: Save button in change reason modal
steps.visual_check: Success toast. Version sidebar now shows 'v2' (active, with purple left border) and 'v1'. Active version badge shows 'v2'. Change reason 'Added financial specialisation...' visible on v2 entry

steps.target: Version v1 in version history sidebar
steps.expected_result: Diff view shown between v1 and v2
steps.visual_check: Diff view visible showing additions in green background and deletions in red background with +/- prefix symbols. The added 'You specialise in financial insights.' line highlighted in green

steps.expected_result: Version restored — v3 created as a copy of v1's content
steps.visual_check: Success toast 'Version restored. Now active: v3'. Version sidebar shows v3 (active), v2, v1. Change reason on v3 shows 'Restored from version 1'

steps.expected_result: List page shows the prompt with active version v3
Prompt visible with 'v3' in active version column

journeys.name: Prompt Editor Variable Autocomplete
journeys.description: Test the {{ variable autocomplete feature in the prompt editor (E5c-3 AC-5)

steps.visual_check: Autocomplete popover visible below cursor showing variables grouped by source type: System (today, currentUser.name, currentUser.role, company.name, company.baseCurrency), DB Fields, Page Fields, Custom. Each variable shows name and source type badge
System prompt textarea shows 'Hello {{company.name}}' with the {{company.name}} portion potentially highlighted with purple background tint

journeys.name: Prompt Test Render Panel
journeys.description: Test the 'Test Prompt' feature that renders a prompt with sample variables (E5c-3 AC-5e)

steps.target: First prompt row (any seeded prompt with variables)
steps.expected_result: Prompt editor loads for the selected prompt
Test panel opens (slide-in sheet or expandable section)
steps.visual_check: Test Prompt panel visible with variable input fields auto-populated from the prompt's bound variables. Each field shows variable name and source type badge. 'Render' button visible

Output section shows rendered system prompt and user template in mono font cards with green left border. Variables replaced with resolved values. If unresolved variables exist, amber warning showing count

journeys.name: Agent Configuration CRUD Lifecycle
journeys.description: View agent list, create a new agent, edit with tabs (tools, guardrails, triggers), and verify business rules (E5c-4 AC-1, AC-2)

steps.visual_check: T1 Entity List with 'Agent Configuration' heading. Columns: name (mono, bold), display name, assigned model (or 'Auto-routed'), assigned prompt (mono), tool count (mono), routing tags (purple badges), max turns (mono), active status. At least 10 seeded agents visible

steps.expected_result: Form has tabs: Main, Tools, Guardrails, Triggers
steps.visual_check: Agent form with tab navigation (Main/Tools/Guardrails/Triggers). Main tab active showing: Name, Display Name, Description, Model dropdown, Prompt dropdown, Routing Tags, Max Turns slider/input, Active toggle

steps.expected_result: Form populated with model and prompt dropdowns selected
Tools tab shows JSON editor for tools array

steps.visual_check: Guardrails tab with structured fields: canRead (tag input), canWrite (tag input), requiresApproval toggle, blockedOperations (tag input), dataScope dropdown

steps.expected_result: Guardrails configured with tags and toggles

steps.visual_check: Success toast 'Agent created'. Page shows saved agent detail. Breadcrumbs: 'AI Administration > Agent Configuration > E2E Test Agent'

steps.expected_result: Agent list shows the new agent
New agent visible in list with correct columns

journeys.name: Skill Pack Manager — Grouped View & Activation Toggle
journeys.description: View skills grouped by module in accordion, toggle skill activation, search skills (E5c-4 AC-3, AC-6)

steps.expected_result: Skill Pack Manager loads with grouped accordion view
steps.visual_check: Skill Pack Manager page with skills grouped by moduleKey in accordion sections. First module expanded. Each skill shown as a card with: name (mono, bold), display name, description, trigger phrases (blue pills), negative triggers (red pills), orchestration pattern badge (purple), priority (mono), active toggle. Search bar visible at top. 'Test Trigger' button visible

steps.expected_result: Multiple module sections visible (ar, ap, finance, sales, etc.) as collapsible accordions with module key heading badges
Second module section expands showing its skills

steps.target: Active toggle on any skill card
steps.expected_result: Skill deactivated — toggle switches off, toast 'Skill {name} deactivated'
steps.visual_check: Toast notification visible confirming skill deactivation. Toggle switch in off position

steps.expected_result: Skill reactivated — toggle switches on, toast 'Skill {name} activated'
Skills filtered — only skills matching 'overdue' in name/description visible across all modules
Only skills with 'overdue' in their name or description are shown
steps.visual_check: Filtered view showing only overdue-related skills. Non-matching modules may be hidden or empty

journeys.name: Skill Edit Form & Test Trigger Panel
journeys.description: Edit a skill's details and test the L0>L1>L2 trigger routing simulation (E5c-4 AC-4, AC-5)

steps.target: Any skill card (e.g., first skill in the AR module)
steps.visual_check: Skill form with tabs: Main, Triggers, Content, Schema. Main tab showing: name (mono), display name, description, category dropdown, module key dropdown, output type dropdown, priority input, active toggle

steps.expected_result: Triggers tab shows trigger phrases (blue pills), negative triggers (red pills), context required (grey pills)
steps.visual_check: Triggers tab with tag inputs: trigger phrases shown as blue pill badges, negative triggers as red pill badges, context required as grey pill badges. Add/remove functionality on each

steps.expected_result: Content tab shows large mono font textarea with full skillContent (SKILL.md)
Test Trigger panel opens (sheet slide-in from right)
steps.visual_check: Test Trigger sheet visible with a text input for typing trigger phrases, and a results area below

steps.target: Test / Run button in trigger panel
steps.visual_check: Results showing: L0 matched module (e.g. 'ar') with confidence score, L1 matched skill name with confidence score, L2 required tools list and first 200 chars of skill content preview. Results appear within 3 seconds

journeys.name: Create Scheduled Automation with Steps
journeys.description: Create a new scheduled automation with multiple steps, cron configuration, and variable bindings (E5c-5 AC-1, AC-2, AC-3, AC-4, AC-5, AC-6)

steps.visual_check: T1 Entity List with 'Automations' heading. Columns: name (bold), trigger type badge (purple Scheduled/blue Event/amber Chain/grey Manual), schedule (human-readable cron), step count (mono), last run status (StatusBadge), last run time (relative), active toggle. At least 1 seeded automation visible. Empty state or data visible

Automation builder with sections: Header (name, description), Trigger (radio group: Scheduled/Event/Manual), Steps (empty with 'Add Step' button), Chain config, Notification config, Budget config. Concept D styling

input_data.Name: E2E Weekly Summary
input_data.Description: Weekly summary automation created during E2E testing

steps.target: Scheduled radio button in trigger type
steps.expected_result: CronBuilder component appears with schedule fields
steps.visual_check: CronBuilder visible with fields: minute, hour, day of month, month, day of week checkboxes. Preset buttons: 'Every minute', 'Hourly', 'Daily at midnight', 'Weekdays at 9 AM', 'Weekly on Monday'. Timezone selector with default 'Europe/London'. Human-readable preview below
Cron fields show: minute=0, hour=9, day-of-month=*, month=*, day-of-week=Mon-Fri checked. Human-readable preview shows 'At 9:00 AM, Monday through Friday'. Raw cron input shows '0 9 * * 1-5'

steps.expected_result: First step card appears with step number 1
steps.visual_check: Step card visible with: step number circle badge '1', agent dropdown (empty), goal textarea (empty), drag handle on left, delete button on right. Vertical connector line from step to 'Add Step' below

input_data.Goal: Analyse all transactions for {{company.name}} this week. Summarise key metrics including revenue, expenses, and anomalies.

steps.expected_result: Step 1 configured with agent selected from dropdown and goal text entered
Second step card appears with step number 2

input_data.Goal: Draft a weekly summary email based on the analysis results.

steps.expected_result: Input configuration panel expands showing JSON editor and 'Use Previous Step Output' button

steps.target: Use Previous Step Output shortcut button
steps.expected_result: inputConfig auto-populated with PREVIOUS_STEP source referencing step 1
steps.visual_check: Input config JSON editor shows populated config referencing step 1 output. Two step cards visible connected by vertical line, both with agent selections and goal text

Success toast 'Automation created'. Page navigates to edit page of the new automation. All configured data visible: name, scheduled trigger with cron preview, 2 steps with agents and goals, budget values

steps.expected_result: Automation list shows the new automation with 'Scheduled' badge, '2' step count, and active toggle on

journeys.name: Automation Chain Configuration & Notifications
journeys.description: Configure chain-to-next-automation and notification settings, verify circular chain rejection (E5c-5 AC-7, AC-8)

steps.target: E2E Weekly Summary row (created in j10)

steps.expected_result: Chain dropdown appears showing other active automations

steps.visual_check: Notification section showing: recipient input, channel checkboxes (In-App, Email), notify on success toggle, notify on failure toggle. Chain section shows selected target automation

steps.expected_result: Automation updated with chain and notification config
steps.visual_check: Success toast. Chain configuration visible. Notification configuration visible

journeys.name: Automation Run Now & Real-Time Status
journeys.description: Manually trigger an automation and observe the run appearing in the list (E5c-5 AC-9, E5c-6 AC-1)

steps.target: Run Now button / action for the seeded 'Daily AR Aging Summary' automation
steps.visual_check: Confirmation dialog asking 'Run this automation now?' with Confirm and Cancel buttons

steps.target: Confirm button in Run Now dialog
steps.expected_result: Automation triggered — toast 'Automation started' with link to run detail
steps.visual_check: Toast notification 'Automation started' with clickable link to view the run. Automation list refreshes — last run status may show 'Running' (amber) or 'Pending' (grey)

steps.expected_result: Automation Runs list page loads showing the new run
steps.visual_check: Automation Runs list with the newly triggered run at top. Columns visible: automation name, trigger type badge, triggered by (showing manual user), started at, duration, status badge, total tokens (mono), total cost (mono)

Run detail page with: run summary header (automation name, run ID in mono, status badge, triggered by, timestamps), metrics cards row (total tokens, total cost, steps completed/total, duration), step-by-step timeline with status indicators (green/red/amber/grey circles). Purple connector lines between steps

journeys.name: Automation Run History Filters
journeys.description: Test run history list filtering by status, date range, and automation name (E5c-6 AC-1, AC-2)

steps.expected_result: Automation Runs list loads with all runs
Filter controls visible: status multi-select, date range inputs (From/To), automation name dropdown, Clear Filters button
steps.visual_check: Filter bar above the run list table with: status dropdown showing checkboxes for PENDING/RUNNING/COMPLETED/FAILED/CANCELLED, two date inputs, automation filter dropdown, Clear Filters button

steps.expected_result: List filters to show only COMPLETED and FAILED runs
Only runs with COMPLETED or FAILED status badges visible
steps.visual_check: Filtered run list showing only green 'Completed' and red 'Failed' status badges. No 'Running', 'Pending', or 'Cancelled' rows

steps.expected_result: All filters reset, full run list visible
List filters to runs for that automation only
All visible runs show 'Daily AR Aging Summary' in automation name column

journeys.name: Run Detail — Step Timeline & Expandable Details
journeys.description: View run detail page with step-by-step timeline, expand steps to see input/output JSON, verify failed step UI (E5c-6 AC-3, AC-4)

steps.target: Any run row (preferably a COMPLETED or FAILED run)

steps.expected_result: Header shows: automation name (linked), run ID in mono, large status badge, triggered by, started/completed timestamps
4 metrics cards visible: Total Tokens (with input/output breakdown), Total Cost, Steps (completed/total), Duration
steps.visual_check: 4 metrics cards in a row with large mono font numbers. Cards have 12px radius and subtle shadow. Token card shows input/output breakdown below. Steps card shows progress bar

steps.expected_result: Vertical timeline visible with status indicators (coloured circles) per step, connected by purple line. Each step shows: step number, agent name, status badge, duration, token count

steps.visual_check: Expanded step showing: goal text in subtle background card, Input Data as collapsible JSON viewer (mono font, with copy button), Output Data as collapsible JSON viewer, model used (mono), turns count, token breakdown (input/output), timing details

steps.expected_result: JSON viewer expands/collapses showing formatted JSON with syntax highlighting
JSON copied to clipboard (toast or visual confirmation)
Step details collapse back to summary view

journeys.name: Failed Run — View Error & Retry from Failed Step
journeys.description: View a failed run's error details and retry from the failed step (E5c-6 AC-4, AC-5)
journeys.preconditions: Requires a FAILED automation run in the database. If none exists, this journey tests the UI structure only

steps.expected_result: List filters to FAILED runs only
Run detail page loads for the failed run
steps.visual_check: Run detail with red 'Failed' status badge. Error banner visible: red-bordered alert card with error message text. 'Retry from Failed Step' primary button visible

steps.expected_result: The failed step has: red circle indicator, red left-border accent on expanded card, error message in red alert box. Subsequent steps show grey 'Skipped' with italic label
steps.visual_check: Step timeline showing: completed steps with green circles, one failed step with red circle and red border accent showing error message, subsequent steps greyed out with 'Skipped (previous step failed)' italic label
Confirmation dialog explaining: 'This will create a new run starting from step N. Previous step outputs will be preserved.' with Confirm and Cancel buttons

steps.expected_result: Retry initiated — toast 'Retry started' with link to new run, page navigates to new run detail
steps.visual_check: New run detail page showing the retry run. Run metadata footer shows retryOfRunId linking to the original failed run

journeys.name: Sidebar Navigation — All AI Admin Pages Accessible
journeys.description: Verify all AI Admin navigation entries work and lead to the correct pages

steps.expected_result: App shell loads with sidebar navigation
AI Admin section visible in sidebar with Brain icon. Sub-items: Dashboard, Model Registry, Prompt Templates, Agents, Skills, Automations, Automation Runs
steps.visual_check: Sidebar showing AI Admin navigation section with all 7 sub-items, each with its icon (Brain, Cpu, FileCode, Bot, Wand2, Workflow, History)
Automation Runs page loaded successfully from sidebar navigation. Active sidebar item highlighted with purple background + white text per Concept D
